title: Alyssoides utriculata
taxonomy: Alyssoides utriculata is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is also known as the bladder Alyssum. This plant is classified as a member of the genus Alyssoides and is closely related to other species within the same genus. Alyssoides utriculata is a low-growing, hairy perennial that produces small white flowers and has a unique bladder-like structure on its stems. The species is also known for its ability to thrive in dry, rocky areas.
